title: Chapter 248
content:
Chapter 248: Chapter 248 : Reminder of her Sins

She pressed her breasts tightly with both hands. Blood still pooled beneath her, a small dark lake spreading across the cold stone. The torn runic gear hung open just enough to free her tits from its hold, the fabric split and useless.

But her healing was already at work. The cuts were closing. Slowly. Painfully. But closing.

Jax didn’t care.

He dug his sword into the ground and walked past her. His grin widened seeing the cycle play out.

Pain, then healing. Damage, then restoration. Whether it was her celestial power or Jenny’s blessed shielding doing the work didn’t matter. Either way, it made his revenge infinitely more satisfying.

A body that fixes itself is a body that can be broken again.

He stopped beside one of the glowing trees and ran his hand across its bark. His voice carried across the dungeon like a blade being dragged across stone.

"I won’t forgive you for what you’ve done. For ruining a happy family. For making a girl’s life a living hell." His fingers wrapped around a thick vine hanging from the branches. "I’m not naive like that girl who forgave you so easily."

He planted one foot against the trunk, gripped the vine with both hands, and pulled. His muscles strained. The vine resisted. Then surrendered. It tore free from the tree with a sound like tendons snapping.

He walked back toward Cleenah, dragging the vine behind him.

"And I won’t let you forget what sins you’ve committed. I will carve a pain so brutal into you that every time you close your eyes, you’ll remember exactly what you’ve done."

He stopped in front of her.

"Now strip off completely. Before I have to do it myself." His sword gleamed in the lantern light. "And just so you know, when I’m angry, my precision in swordsmanship tends to suffer. The more you test my patience, the more accuracy I lose. And the more accuracy I lose, the more skin and blood comes off with the fabric."

She didn’t test him.

Her trembling hands reached for the torn gear at her chest. She pulled the fabric apart, tearing it further, then dragged it off her shoulders. The runic material slid down her arms and pooled at her waist, leaving only the bottom half.

Her healed breasts were immediately hidden behind her crossed arms. Shame burning hotter than the wounds had.

Jax stepped beside her. Deliberately. His boot came down in the pool of blood beneath her feet.

The dark liquid scattered outward. Droplets hitting her ankles. Her knees.

"Look at this blood." His voice was quiet. Dangerous. "What do you feel when you see it?"

She didn’t answer.

"It’s the same. The same blood you spilled blindly. Not just Lilith’s mother. Not just your own brother. But countless others. Demihumans whose full stories you never bothered to learn before ending their lives."

He crouched. Dragged his finger through the red pool. Held it up.

"Can you spot the difference between yours and theirs?"

Silence.

Jax stood and reached for the zipper of his own runic gear. The sound of the fabric parting cut through the quiet. He pulled the gear off from the bottom, letting it drop to the ground. Leaving nothing but his underwear.

Cleenah didn’t see it. Her eyes were locked on the blood beneath her. Her mind spiraling through faces she had forgotten. Families she had never considered. Lives she had stamped out with the certainty of someone who believed they were saving the world.

Her fist clenched.

"I will pay for my sins." Her voice was raw. Broken but resolute. "Punish me to your heart’s content. If that’s what it takes for forgiveness."

She looked up.

And found Jax standing before her. Underwear gone. His cock free, hard, the tip aimed at her like an accusation.

A pause. "They didn’t get the mercy that you got."
